steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The subject is “more writers from Guinea, including Diallo.” The head noun “writers” is plural. The phrase “including Diallo” is parenthetical and doesn't affect the count.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: the verb must agree in number with the head noun of the subject, not with intervening phrases.
  • Choice A — "is beginning": singular present progressive; does not agree with the plural subject "writers." ✗
  • Choice B — "has begun": singular present perfect; does not agree with the plural subject. ✗
  • Choice C — "have begun": plural present perfect; correctly agrees with the plural head noun "writers." ✓
  • Choice D — "begins": singular simple present; does not agree with the plural subject. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
